findings.validated_statement: DB_GLOBAL_HISTORICO.xlsx exists as an unreferenced workbook and Hoja1 has the BASE 12-column schema, but row counts are header-sensitive and retirement cannot be inferred because DB coverage is incomplete for the historical range checked.
evidence: DB_GLOBAL_HISTORICO.xlsx Hoja1 max_row=141886, 141885 data rows
No runtime/script references found outside research material
public.fact_stock_venta date range from read-only profile: 2026-05-02 to 2026-06-07
contradictions: Claim used 141886 as rows without distinguishing header
DB retirement condition is not met by current fact_stock_venta date coverage
findings.required_next_action: Confirm operational use with Bastian and compare against authoritative DB coverage before any retirement proposal.

findings.validated_statement: DB_GLOBAL_INVENTARIO.xlsx BASE has four formula-derived columns (COD_RT, NOMBRE_LOCAL_RR, OTROS, duplicados) and eight direct source columns; the loader reads saved values rather than evaluating Excel formulas.
evidence: BASE table Tabla1 A1:L43636
Workbook XML formulas in columns I:J:K:L
Formula caches present for I,J,L and absent for K in XML inspection
scripts/load_fact_from_excel.py reads workbook values through pandas/openpyxl path
findings.required_next_action: Keep as validated memory; any offload requires parity tests before implementation.

findings.validated_statement: The BASE duplicados column is ignored by the loader; deduplication is performed in Python and enforced again by the DB upsert conflict key.
evidence: scripts/load_fact_from_excel.py source_check treats extra columns as warnings
scripts/load_fact_from_excel.py drop_duplicates path
ON CONFLICT (fecha, cod_rt, sku, marca)
DB constraint on public.fact_stock_venta (fecha, cod_rt, sku, marca)
findings.required_next_action: Treat removal as an operational workbook decision, not a code-approved change.

findings.validated_statement: COD_RT depends on an Excel XLOOKUP over RUTA_RUTERO for each BASE row, so stale saved formula values can be ingested silently by the loader.
evidence: COD_RT formula uses XLOOKUP with CADENA, COD B2B/N_LOCAL, CLIENTE/MARCA conditions
BASE has 43635 data rows
RUTA_RUTERO has 3568 data rows
No formula-recency or formula-cache validation in scripts/load_fact_from_excel.py
findings.required_next_action: Future guard should validate formula/cache freshness and operator workflow; mtime alone is not enough.

findings.validated_statement: DB_GLOBAL_HISTORICO.xlsx is not referenced by runtime/loaders and resembles a manual archive, but its construction method and operational authority are not proven.
evidence: No app/scripts/tests references found
Hoja1 matches BASE 12-column layout
File modified earlier than DB_GLOBAL_INVENTARIO.xlsx
contradictions: The statement that it was built by accumulating BASE copies remains an inference
findings.required_next_action: Ask Bastian whether this workbook is an authoritative manual archive.

findings.validated_statement: Computing COD_RT/NOMBRE_LOCAL_RR in code is plausible, but the safest parity source is the same workbook RUTA_RUTERO or a versioned local snapshot, not a live public.ruta_rutero lookup without temporal controls.
evidence: public.ruta_rutero exists with 3568 rows
Workbook RUTA_RUTERO has 3568 data rows
public.ruta_rutero row_hash distinct count differs slightly from row count
Formula keys require CADENA + N_LOCAL/COD_B2B + MARCA/CLIENTE alignment
contradictions: Live DB ruta can be fresher than the workbook period and may create temporal mismatch
findings.required_next_action: Design a parity test using workbook RUTA_RUTERO first, then evaluate a versioned ruta snapshot.

findings.validated_statement: CUMPLIMIENTO_FRECUENCIA.xlsx has seven sheets and the committed CG loader consumes only DB (KPIONE), DB (KPIONE2.0), and DB (POWER_APP).
evidence: Workbook sheet profile: DB (KPIONE), DB (KPIONE2.0), DB (POWER_APP), DESARROLLO_CONTROL_GESTION, Tabla1, OUT, gg
scripts/load_control_gestion_raw_v17.py SOURCE_TO_SHEET maps only KPIONE, KPIONE2, POWER_APP
findings.required_next_action: Document sheet roles before any workbook governance change.

findings.validated_statement: DESARROLLO_CONTROL_GESTION and OUT are not consumed by committed loader code or runtime code found in this pass.
evidence: SOURCE_TO_SHEET excludes DESARROLLO_CONTROL_GESTION and OUT
Search across app/scripts/tests found no runtime consumer of those sheet names
findings.required_next_action: Confirm manual usage with Bastian before treating these sheets as removable.

findings.validated_statement: OUT resembles a manual wide-format CG artifact and is not consumed by runtime, but whether it predates or still parallels DB-backed CG reporting requires business confirmation.
evidence: OUT sheet shape: 3440 x 34
cg_mart fact/mv weekly objects exist and are read by app/db.py
No loader/runtime reference to OUT sheet name
contradictions: Manual predecessor and ongoing parallel tracking are interpretations, not directly proven
findings.required_next_action: Ask Bastian whether OUT remains operationally used.

findings.validated_statement: The ruta loader computes row_hash as an analysis fingerprint and explicitly does not use it as the upsert key.
evidence: scripts/load_ruta_rutero_from_excel.py row_hash comment says it is not the UPSERT key
public.ruta_rutero upsert key is (source, source_row)
row_hash written to public.ruta_rutero and cg_core.ruta_rutero_load_rows
findings.required_next_action: Keep as validated memory; do not remove without consumer and audit policy review.

findings.validated_statement: The ruta loader writes the current routing table plus full load history and batch metadata; DB profile confirms accumulated route load batches.
evidence: Loader targets public.ruta_rutero, cg_core.ruta_rutero_load_rows, cg_core.ruta_rutero_load_batch
DB profile: ruta_rutero_load_batch rows=19
DB profile: ruta_rutero_load_rows rows=64346
findings.required_next_action: Use DB consumers and retention evidence before proposing cleanup.

findings.validated_statement: No app/runtime consumer of row_hash was found, but cg_core.ruta_rutero_load_rows itself is consumed by DB views, so the history layer is not only manual DB auditing.
evidence: No row_hash references in app/db.py or screens/services
DB profile found ruta_rutero_load_rows consumers in cg_core.v_rr_frecuencia_base_resuelta_v2 and cg_mart.v_cg_ruta_duplicados_auditoria_v2
No row_hash definition hits found in the DB profile
contradictions: History table has DB view consumers, not just manual audit use
findings.required_next_action: Separate row_hash consumer analysis from route history table consumer analysis.

findings.validated_statement: The CG raw loader is a large, highly coupled script with 54 functions and three raw source targets, but line count is 2024 in the current checked file, not 2023.
evidence: scripts/load_control_gestion_raw_v17.py line count checked as 2024
Function count checked as 54
Targets cg_raw.kpione_raw, cg_raw.kpione2_raw, cg_raw.power_app_raw and cg_audit.batch_registry
contradictions: Exact line count differs by one
findings.required_next_action: Use as complexity signal only; no refactor authorized.

findings.validated_statement: The CG raw loader uses source sheet hash checks and batch_registry, so unchanged sheets can be skipped while changed sheets create batch records.
evidence: scripts/load_control_gestion_raw_v17.py hash/check logic
DB profile: cg_audit.batch_registry has 37 batches across DB (KPIONE), DB (KPIONE2.0), DB (POWER_APP)
No loader execution was performed
findings.required_next_action: Retain as pipeline behavior memory; retention decisions require DB policy.

findings.validated_statement: app/db.py does not read cg_raw payload_json directly, but DB views in cg_core/cg_mart do read payload_json, so payload_json is not a zero-consumer field.
evidence: No app/db.py raw payload_json consumer found
DB profile found payload_json in cg_core.v_cg_evento_scope, cg_core.v_cg_evidencia_unificada, and multiple cg_mart audit views
Untracked SQL drafts also reference payload_json but are not authority
contradictions: The application-level zero-consumer conclusion omits DB view consumers
findings.required_next_action: Reject any payload_json removal proposal until DB view consumers are redesigned and migrated.

findings.validated_statement: The incremental refresh rebuilds daily and weekly mart facts through staged validation before commit; the data mutation transaction commits before a separate best-effort ANALYZE step.
evidence: scripts/refresh_control_gestion_v2_incremental.py stages daily and weekly data
Validation failures before conn.commit roll back
conn.commit occurs before post-commit ANALYZE; ANALYZE failures produce warning after rollback of analyze transaction
contradictions: Any broad claim that every post-step failure leaves a partially updated state is overstated; analyze is post-commit only
findings.required_next_action: Document commit/analyze boundary; no runtime change authorized.

findings.validated_statement: The ten named app/db.py functions have no static callers in app, scripts, or tests, and no known dynamic lookup pattern was found in this pass.
evidence: Search found only definitions for the ten candidate functions
Search for getattr, __all__, globals, locals, importlib found no relevant dynamic db.py dispatch
Previously recorded static-only functions were rechecked
findings.required_next_action: Keep as static-only candidates; deletion requires a separate approved cleanup and runtime smoke.

findings.validated_statement: app/db.py is a large SQL-bearing module with 80 f-string SQL blocks, 35 JOIN clauses, and 54 os.getenv calls, but CTE count and the exact CG_V2 constant list in the Claude claim were inaccurate.
evidence: app/db.py checked as 5348 lines
Counted 80 triple f-string SQL blocks, 35 JOIN clauses, 22 WITH patterns, 54 os.getenv calls
Actual CG_V2 constants differ from the claimed names
contradictions: CTE count checked as 22, not 15
Not all claimed CG_V2_* names exist
findings.required_next_action: Use corrected constant list for any future runbook or probe design.

findings.validated_statement: Home.py defers app/db.py, exports, and screen imports inside main(), so those modules are not imported by Home.py at module import time.
evidence: app/Home.py imports db, exports, and screen modules inside main()
No equivalent module-level imports found outside main()
findings.required_next_action: Record as import behavior; no AGENTS.md change authorized in this phase.

findings.validated_statement: The ten static-only functions can be grouped conceptually, but whether they are abandoned, incomplete, or intentionally diagnostic is an interpretation.
evidence: Function names and SQL shapes suggest coverage-audit and CG diagnostic groups
No static callers found
No runtime execution was performed
contradictions: Abandoned-stub language is not proven by static analysis
findings.required_next_action: Ask Bastian or inspect historical commits before classifying intent.

findings.validated_statement: The project references public, cg_raw, cg_core, cg_mart, and cg_audit objects, but app/db.py does not cover the full DB dependency graph because DB views connect raw/core/mart layers.
evidence: Schema-prefixed references found across app/db.py, scripts, and SQL drafts
DB profile covered 11 allowlisted relations
DB definition hits show cg_core/cg_mart views consume raw payloads and route load rows
contradictions: cg_raw is not only write-only in the DB graph; it is read through DB views
findings.required_next_action: Build an authoritative object catalog before object cleanup.

findings.validated_statement: app/db.py does define nine CG_V2_* environment-backed constants, but several names listed by Claude do not exist and the actual defaults differ.
evidence: Actual constants: CG_V2_SCOPE_VIEW, CG_V2_DETALLE_VIEW, CG_V2_OUT_WEEKLY_VIEW, CG_V2_MULTI_MARCAJE_VIEW, CG_V2_DAILY_EVIDENCE_VIEW, CG_V2_ROUTE_FREQ_RESUELTA_VIEW, CG_V2_RUTA_DUP_VIEW, CG_V2_FUERA_CRUCE_REAL_VIEW, CG_V2_SIN_BATCH_RUTA_VIEW
Claimed constants such as CG_V2_PRECEDENCIA_VIEW and CG_V2_BASE_MV were not found
contradictions: Exact claimed constant list is wrong
findings.required_next_action: Reformulate any env probe using the actual constant list.

findings.validated_statement: The app does not read cg_raw directly, but the DB view layer does, so raw is not decoupled from runtime-visible CG outputs.
evidence: No app/db.py raw table read found
DB profile found payload_json and ruta_rutero_load_rows references in DB views
cg_mart outputs read by app/db.py depend on upstream core/mart view logic
contradictions: Raw-layer decoupling is overstated when DB views feed app-facing facts
findings.required_next_action: Treat raw retention as DB-architecture work, not a simple app cleanup.

findings.validated_statement: The inventory workbook has formula and freshness dependencies that make manual process errors plausible, but the exact seven-step operational workflow and timing estimates require Bastian confirmation.
evidence: Formula dependencies confirmed in BASE
No formula freshness preflight in load_fact_from_excel.py
No runtime or operator workflow observation performed
contradictions: Manual step count and 10-60 second recalc window are not independently measured
findings.required_next_action: Validate actual operator workflow with Bastian before creating process gates.

findings.validated_statement: OTROS depends on FOCO_CLIENTE lookup logic and appears lower priority than COD_RT, but hit rate and best target representation need parity evidence.
evidence: FOCO_CLIENTE sheet max_row=849
OTROS formula uses FECHA + CADENA + N_LOCAL + SKU
K-column formula cache missing in XML inspection
contradictions: The 2 percent non-empty hit-rate was not confirmed from cached OTROS values
findings.required_next_action: Revisit after COD_RT parity design; do not create a FOCO_CLIENTE DB table from this evidence alone.

findings.validated_statement: The SQL directory contains committed draft files and untracked 09/10 drafts, but live-object status cannot be inferred only from filenames or git status.
evidence: sql/01-08 present plus untracked sql/09 and sql/10
Untracked 09/10 remain outside this commit
DB profile was limited to C001 allowlisted relations and did not execute SQL drafts
contradictions: No-evidence-of-live-objects is not the same as proof they were never applied
findings.required_next_action: Keep SQL governance in backlog; compare against catalog/migrations before cleanup.

findings.validated_statement: DB_GLOBAL_INVENTARIO has an internal DB_HISTORICA sheet and DB_GLOBAL_HISTORICO has an external Hoja1 archive, but internal DB_HISTORICA does not share the BASE 12-column schema.
evidence: DB_HISTORICA max_row=244009 and table Tabla4 has 10 columns
External Hoja1 has the BASE-style 12 columns
Internal DB_HISTORICA columns differ from BASE and omit COD_RT/NOMBRE_LOCAL_RR/OTROS/duplicados
contradictions: Both-have-identical-BASE-schema claim is false for internal DB_HISTORICA
findings.required_next_action: Reformulate historical archive map with distinct schemas.

findings.validated_statement: There are multiple historical stock/sales layers, but they are not schema-equivalent and DB fact coverage currently does not cover the same historical span as the external archive.
evidence: Internal DB_HISTORICA data rows=244008 with 10-column structure
External Hoja1 data rows=141885 with BASE-style structure
public.fact_stock_venta rows=112250 and fecha range 2026-05-02 to 2026-06-07
Hoja1 date range checked as 2026-03-03 to 2026-04-06
contradictions: Source-of-truth risk is real, but equality/redundancy is not proven
findings.required_next_action: Map date ranges and business use before any retirement decision.

findings.validated_statement: The current DB evidence does not support retiring DB_GLOBAL_HISTORICO.xlsx, because public.fact_stock_venta does not cover the external historical workbook date range observed in this validation.
evidence: public.fact_stock_venta fecha range: 2026-05-02 to 2026-06-07
DB_GLOBAL_HISTORICO Hoja1 date range: 2026-03-03 to 2026-04-06
contradictions: Condition for retirement is false in current aggregate evidence
findings.required_next_action: Do not propose retirement now; require Bastian decision and fuller lineage map.

findings.validated_statement: payload_json cannot be treated as removable based on current evidence because multiple DB views read it; the 40-60 percent write-size saving estimate is unsupported here.
evidence: DB profile found payload_json references in cg_core and cg_mart view definitions
No row-level size samples were collected
No DB write or migration was performed
contradictions: Zero-consumer prerequisite fails
Storage-saving estimate was not measured
findings.required_next_action: Reject removal recommendation until full DB dependency redesign exists.

R01_remove_duplicados.reason: Technically redundant for the loader, but manual workbook use was not validated.

R02_mtime_preflight.reason: mtime alone is weak; use formula/cache/freshness plus operator-confirmed workflow.

R03_document_cg_sheet_roles.reason: Loader consumes only the three DB source sheets; manual sheets require business context.

R04_ruta_retention_52_loads.reason: DB has 19 route batches and route history has DB view consumers; retention window needs policy, not an arbitrary count.

R05_cg_raw_retention_12_weeks.reason: cg_raw feeds DB views; retention needs reconstructability and storage evidence.

R06_cg_env_startup_probe.reason: The claimed constant list was wrong; future probe must use actual constants and avoid startup UX risk.

R07_python_cod_rt_offload.reason: Start with same-workbook RUTA_RUTERO for exact parity, then evaluate versioned snapshot; live DB can introduce temporal mismatch.

R08_remove_payload_json.reason: DB views consume payload_json; zero-consumer prerequisite is false.

R09_zero_caller_cleanup.reason: Static-only candidates validated, but deletion needs separate approval and runtime smoke.

R10_retire_historical_excel.reason: public.fact_stock_venta does not cover the external historical workbook date range observed.

non_authority_notice: This matrix validates research claims only. It authorizes no runtime, loader, SQL, DB, data, or evidence changes.
